definition: description and explanation of designed-in testability features which may be needed and appropriate for customer testing of a die device

definition_ml.en: description and explanation of designed-in testability features which may be needed and appropriate for customer testing of a die device

note: Examples of built-in testability features include redundancy, control fuses, error correction, ad-hoc structured boundary scan and built-in self-test. Such information shall only be supplied when it does not infringe the intellectual property rights of the supplier

note_ml.en: Examples of built-in testability features include redundancy, control fuses, error correction, ad-hoc structured boundary scan and built-in self-test. Such information shall only be supplied when it does not infringe the intellectual property rights of the supplier
